'''ROX-N''' was a [[Droid/Legends|droid]] owned by the [[Star Tours/Legends|Star Tours]] travel agency. At the time of the [[Raid at Spaceport THX1138]], the droid was being stored in a shipping crate.<ref name="EEReview">{{EndorExpress|url=star-wars-adventures/star-tours-2/2017/04/review-star-tours-laventure-continue|text=Review: Star Tours: l'Aventure Continue}}</ref> Years later, she staffed an information kiosk for the [[Astroport Interstellar Services]] section of a [[Tomorrowland Starport|starport]] owned by Star Tours. She was very fond of [[RX-24/Legends|RX-24]].<ref name="SWI 104">{{InsiderCite|104|Star Tours: Taking Off on a Star Tour|''Star Tours'': Taking Off on a Star Tour}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The character of ROX-N is exclusive to the original [[Star Tours (real-world)|''Star Tours'']] attraction at the [[Disneyland Paris]] theme park in [[Wikipedia:France|France]], serving as a multi-lingual host to the post-show arcade ''L'Astroport Services Interstellaires'', which once featured many custom games.<ref name="SWI 104" /> She was an oracle of the park, dispensing fortunes to passersby, but with declining interest in the arcade, her functions were reduced to the multi-lingual greetings spiel.<ref name="EE Database">{{EndorExpress|url=database/rox-n|text=Database: ROX-N}}</ref> ROX-N seemingly swoons over RX-24 as park-goers pass her in the queue.<ref name="SWI 104" />

She reappeared in the new version of the ride, ''[[Star Tours: The Adventures Continue]]'', when it was released in Disneyland Paris on [[March 18]], [[2017]], where she was packed into a shipping container alongside some [[G2 repair droid/Legends|goose droids]], expanding on a concept from the initial release.<ref name="EEReview" />
